title: MacroRegime — Regime-Switching Asset Allocation
short_description: Dual-frequency HMM/GMM regime detection pipeline with causal filtered macro + market features, point-in-time publication lags, and regime-conditional asset allocation backtested via QBacktest.
long_description: MacroRegimePipeline detects market regimes using causally-filtered HMM and GMM models on dual-frequency macro and daily market features. Regime labels are never forward-filled before lag application, preventing PIT leakage. The pipeline benchmarks against 60/40, equal-weight, and risk-parity strategies with identical cost parameters.
technical_details:
## Algorithm & Architecture

MacroRegimePipeline implements a dual-frequency causal regime detection system:

```
PIT macro panel (lag applied by loader)
  → expanding z-score standardization (NOT full-sample — prevents future mean/std leakage)
  → CausalRegimeDetector (macro) → macro_regimes (publication-date indexed)
Daily OHLCV → causal market features (each feature shift(1))
  → expanding z-score standardization
  → CausalRegimeDetector (daily) → market_regimes (daily)
Forward-fill macro regimes to daily AFTER lag application (Pitfall 5 safe)
  → combine: macro takes precedence; market used before macro warm-up
  → month_end_rebalance_dates → weight_schedule → run_strategy_backtest
```

## Causality Chain (Never Reorder)

1. PIT macro panel — publication-date indexed with release lag applied by loader
2. Expanding z-score on macro (no future mean/std)
3. CausalRegimeDetector on macro matrix → macro_regimes
4. Daily market features shifted by 1 bar
5. Expanding z-score on daily features
6. CausalRegimeDetector on daily matrix → market_regimes
7. Forward-fill macro regimes AFTER lag application
8. Combine (macro precedence) → weight schedule → backtest

## Causal Filtered Regimes

`CausalRegimeDetector` wraps hmmlearn/sklearn with a causal oracle guarantee: label at bar t depends only on features[:t+1]. Two separate instances are used for macro and daily — NEVER concatenated (different frequencies create alignment artifacts).

**Label ordering rule**: Regime states ordered ascending by economic observable. State 0 = contraction/stress; State K-1 = expansion/risk-on. Implementation: `permutation = np.argsort(np.argsort(means[:, dim]))`

## Strategy Comparison (from reports/summary.md)

| Strategy | Gross Sharpe | Net Sharpe | Net CI | Sortino | MaxDD | Turnover |
|----------|-------------|-----------|--------|---------|-------|----------|
| Regime | 0.235 | 0.226 | [-0.418, 0.862] | 0.334 | -0.404 | 1.094 |
| 60/40 | 0.023 | 0.022 | [-0.594, 0.656] | 0.032 | -0.298 | 0.108 |
| EqualWeight | 0.087 | 0.086 | [-0.529, 0.649] | 0.136 | -0.291 | 0.097 |
| RiskParity | 1.004 | 0.981 | [0.335, 1.661] | 1.120 | -0.027 | 0.209 |

*HMM/GMM agreement (daily, aligned): 86.0%. K=3 selected by BIC / dwell-time interpretability, NOT by Sharpe (locked anti-feature: selecting K by Sharpe overfits the regime model to the backtest period).*

## Oracle Test Suite (42 tests)

- `test_causal_detector_no_lookahead`: Regime at t uses only features[:t+1]
- `test_pit_lag_applied_before_ffill`: Forward-fill only occurs after lag application (Pitfall 5)
- `test_weight_schedule_sum_to_one`: All weight schedules sum to 1.0 ± 1e-6
- `test_benchmark_cost_parity`: All strategy backtests use identical cost parameters
- `test_hmm_gmm_agreement`: Reports agreement percentage between models
- `test_k_selection_not_by_sharpe`: Anti-feature assertion — K selected by BIC, not backtest performance
